steps.title: Treat the repeated expression as a single unit
steps.content:
The factor $(x^2 - x)$ appears in both terms. Treat it like a variable — call it $u$ for clarity:
$$4u + 2u = 6u$$
Substitute back: $$6(x^2 - x)$$

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• A: $6x^2 - 6x$ — exactly what we derived. That confirms A is correct.
  • B: $6x^2 + 6x$ — wrong sign on the second term. Eliminated.
  • C: $6x$ — missing the $x^2$ term. Eliminated.
  • D: $6x^2$ — missing the $-6x$ term. Eliminated.
